summary: Normal local-access monitoring must use Bambu's one-based plate identifiers, wait briefly for initialized ETA telemetry, and never borrow a stale 3MF.
reason: Display- and app-launched jobs can expose G-code without a current 3MF, while the first MQTT transition can retain stale layer data or report zero remaining time. A regen must preserve honest omission, precise warnings, and a bounded ETA-enrichment wait instead of emitting the prior job's metadata or an avoidably incomplete start notification.
validated_outcome: Display and Handy Benchy runs emitted matching lifecycle pairs without false previews or weights; plate resolved to 1, unavailable metadata named the printer-resident G-code condition, and the Handy run proved ETA telemetry initialized on the next report.
